steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a problem: theater needs dialogue, but the original story conveyed internal thoughts without it. The underlined sentence begins "To transform..." and directly addresses how Yamauchi solved that specific problem. The right answer must describe that solution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'casting decisions': Casting is absent from the underlined sentence entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — 'converting internal monologue into spoken dialogue': The sentence explains precisely how Yamauchi turned the story's internal thoughts into theatrical dialogue — the solution to the stated problem. ✓
  • Choice C — 'literary influence': No literary influence is discussed in the underlined sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'relative fame': Comparisons of fame do not appear in the underlined sentence. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
